paper_title: The success probability in Levine's hat problem, and independent sets in graphs
summary: Conjecture 2.6 (the graph-theoretic restatement of Levine's conjecture, that $\lim_{t\to\infty}\lim_{n\to\infty}\bar{\alpha}(K(n)^{\square t})=0$) remains open as of May 2026. Two subsequent papers address the hat problem using analytic methods: Heilman–Tamuz (arXiv:2503.09042, 2025) obtain an upper bound on the two-player success probability via Chang’s lemma in Boolean harmonic analysis, and Bouquet et al. (arXiv:2508.01737, 2025) develop a geometric/integral framework and derive a 7/20 winning probability for two players. Neither paper resolves the double-limit conjecture on Kneser–Hamming products.

since_posted.title: A Fourier approach to Levine's hat puzzle
since_posted.claim: Provides an upper bound on the success probability in the two-player case using Chang's lemma in Boolean harmonic analysis, offering partial analytic progress toward the conjecture without resolving the full double-limit statement.

since_posted.title: An analytical framework for the Levine hats problem: new strategies, bounds and generalizations
since_posted.authors: C. Bouquet, Salah Chikhi, Timothé Charles, Yang Zhou, Eric Wang
since_posted.claim: Develops a geometric/integral framework for hat-problem strategies, constructs a recursive strategy achieving 7/20 winning probability for two players, and solves a generalization with infinite hat stacks, without addressing the Kneser–Hamming double-limit conjecture.

notes: Conjecture 2.6 is a graph-theoretic restatement of Levine's main conjecture (Conjecture 2.1) via the identity p_intersecting(t,n) = \bar{\alpha}(K(n)^{\square t}). Subsequent work focuses on specific cases (two players) and analytic upper bounds, but the full double-limit conjecture on Kneser–Hamming products remains open. No verified paper directly resolves the graph-theoretic formulation.
